meta.titre: Économie de l'Intention — Analyse des Implémentations Mondiales
meta.reference_theorique: Doc Searls, 'The Intention Economy' (2012), ProjectVRM @ Berkman Klein Center Harvard (2006)

categories.STARTUPS_INTENTION: Startups implémentant la souveraineté des données personnelles
categories.BLOCKCHAIN_DATA: Projets blockchain de marketplace/souveraineté de données
categories.AFRICAN_DATA_ET_CORPORATE_VRM: Data africaine et tentatives corporate VRM

initiatives.concept: Originally a decentralized social media network on Ethereum and IPFS for permanent, censorship-resistant publishing with micropayments via ETH upvotes, enabling user-owned content and data sovereignty through peer-to-peer distribution. Pivoted to public-good infrastructure supporting Ethereum community intelligence: funding rails (Ethereum.Community), global hubs (ETHERLAKEN, Barcelona, Rome), and knowledge preservation (Ethereum TV), emphasizing privacy, decentralization, and community-led progress.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (pivoted from social dapp to Ethereum ecosystem support)
initiatives.funding: No public funding rounds or ICO conducted; planned custom token post-alpha but not executed; non-profit backed by founder Mihai Alisie (Ethereum co-founder).
initiatives.traction: Original social network: alpha 2016, public beta 2018, low traction/no critical mass. Current: 2-10 employees, 1600+ LinkedIn followers, active hubs and projects like Ethereum TV archive; founder speaking at 2025 Ethereum events.
initiatives.raison_echec: Original social dapp: high UX barriers (full Ethereum/IPFS nodes required), gas costs for interactions, slow performance, competition from established platforms, failed to achieve network effects/critical mass despite beta launch.
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Prioritize seamless UX over full decentralization initially (e.g., light clients, abstractions); bootstrap with incentives but ensure low entry barriers; focus on niche sovereignty use cases before broad social; pivot early if no traction, leverage founder network for ecosystem roles; avoid token hype without proven product.

initiatives.fondateurs: Non-profit founded by 70+ organizations (e.g., Business Finland, Sitra, Forum Virium Helsinki) and ~500 individuals (key early figures: Antti 'Jogi' Poikola, Viivi Lähteenoja, Berit Skjernaa, Paul-Olivier Dehaye); evolved from MyData Network (2017)
initiatives.concept: Human-centric personal data management empowering individuals with self-determination and control over their data; promotes ethical data sharing based on explicit consent and trust, shifting from organizational silos to user-controlled flows via operators/data intermediaries, aligning with VRM/intention economy principles by enabling people to set agendas for data use and derive value (per MyData Declaration)
initiatives.statut_detail: Active
initiatives.funding: Non-profit; funded by memberships (~500 members), donations, EU/Nordic grants/projects (e.g., Data Spaces Support Centre, DS4Skills); no VC/equity funding known
initiatives.traction: ~500 members (100+ orgs, 400 individuals from 40+ countries); 1,769 MyData Declaration signatories; thousands in community/Slack; annual MyData Conference (e.g., 2025: 400+ attendees from 40+ countries); dozens of local hubs; EU project partnerships (Fraunhofer, TNO, etc.); MyData Awards
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (active and growing)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Sustain momentum via non-profit structure, global community building (hubs, conferences, Slack), policy advocacy, and grant-funded projects; focus on standards/declarations for interoperability; leverage Nordic/EU support for data sovereignty initiatives

initiatives.concept: Solid (Social Linked Data) is a web decentralization project where users store personal data in secure 'pods' (personal online datastores) they control fully: location, contents, access permissions. Apps request read/write access via authentication; users grant/revoke granular permissions, enabling data sovereignty, portability, and intention-based sharing without vendor lock-in. Built on Linked Data, RDF, W3C standards.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (stewarded by Open Data Institute since Oct 2024; Inrupt active with products/blog 2026)
initiatives.funding: Inrupt (commercial arm): ~$36M+ total (Seed 2018, Series A $30M Dec 2021 led by Forté Ventures w/ Akamai, Glasswing, Allstate, Minderoo; Series B 2022 w/ Accenture); MIT grant Mastercard 2015.
initiatives.traction: Enterprise deployments: Flanders govt (6.5M citizens via Athumi), NHS Greater Manchester pilots, US construction (Graphmetrix), Code for PDX homelessness support. Inrupt pilots w/ BBC, NatWest, Sweden/Argentina/Basque govts. Early revenue $225K (2020).
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (ongoing success in niches)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Prioritize enterprise/govt pilots for critical mass before consumer; leverage open standards + commercial server (like Inrupt ESS) for scalability; stewardship transitions (MIT -> Inrupt -> ODI) sustain momentum; focus on interoperability/privacy regs alignment for adoption.

initiatives.concept: ProjectVRM develops Vendor Relationship Management (VRM) as customer-side counterpart to CRM, promoting 'intention economy' where individuals use tools like intentcasting, personal RFPs, first-person technologies, and standards (e.g., IEEE P7012/MyTerms) for sovereign control over data/relationships with vendors, proving free customers exceed captive ones in value.[ProjectVRM About](https://projectvrm.org/about/)[Wikipedia VRM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vendor_relationship_management)
initiatives.statut_detail: Active
initiatives.funding: 2009 Surdna Foundation grant (amount undisclosed); Customer Commons (2012 spinoff) funds related efforts like First Person Project. No major VC.[ProjectVRM Projects](https://projectvrm.org/projects/)
initiatives.traction: VRM community growth: many dev projects/companies (Azigo, Mydex, etc.), wiki/mailing list, IIW workshops, inspired MyTerms, Visa AI commerce, adblock success (biggest boycott). No user/revenue figures.[Berkman VRM History](https://cyber.harvard.edu/projectvrm/VRM_History)
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (ongoing since 2006)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Prioritize standards/protocols (e.g., MyTerms) and community (workshops, non-profits) for enduring sovereignty tools; academic hosting aids longevity; target practical flips like customer terms over surveillance.[ProjectVRM Intention 2025](https://projectvrm.org/2025/05/16/how-do-we-get-to-the-intention-economy/)

initiatives.concept: Pioneer VRM/personal data sovereignty platform providing a personal cloud (API-of-Me) for individuals to aggregate, manage, control consent, and exchange personal data (identity, financial, health, IoT) with trusted organizations for mutual value, via secure B2B2C enterprise tools emphasizing privacy-by-design, decentralization, and standards compliance (GDPR, Open Banking).
initiatives.statut_detail: Active
initiatives.funding: Pre-Series A AUD $9.4M (2019, cash/IP/assets incl. SVX Group acquisition); seed round (2014, undisclosed); initially self-funded.[FinTech Alliance](https://fintech-alliance.com/news-insights/article/meeco-announces-capital-raise-acquisition-and-european-expansion), [Meeco Bio](https://www.meeco.me/team-members/katryna-dow)
initiatives.traction: Deployments with banks/financial institutions (Australia, Benelux e.g. KBC Bank; Japan DNP); partners incl. VELA Solutions, My Life Capsule; growing revenues/partnerships (2019+); ISO27001, GDPR compliant; active in 2025 conferences/podcasts.[Meeco About](https://www.meeco.me/about-meeco), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/katrynadow), [EIC 2025](https://www.meeco.me/blog/from-fragmented-solutions-to-connected-ecosystems-key-insights-from-eic2025)
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (ongoing operations, recent 2025 activity)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Sustained viability in niche VRM/data sovereignty via B2B2C focus on regulated sectors (finance, telco, govt), standards participation (IEEE), geographic expansion (EU/Japan), acquisitions for tech/revenue, and decentralization pivot aligning with regs/AI era—build interoperable infrastructure over consumer apps for ecosystem scale.

initiatives.fondateurs: Vladislav Kuznetsov (CEO/Founder), Dmitry Martyanov (CTO Co-Founder), Sergey Kolomiets (Head of Investment)
initiatives.concept: Decentralized P2P cryptocurrency exchange (StreamDesk) using smart contracts for direct fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-crypto trades without intermediaries, low fees (0-2%), real-time market rates, KYC compliant. Users control transactions via smart contracts; no mention of intention economy, VRM, or personal data sovereignty features. Ecosystem includes wallet, education, news. [Crypto News Australia review](https://cryptonews.com.au/reviews/streamity-ico-review/), [ChipIn](https://www.chipin.com/streamity-ico-decentralized-cryptocurrency-exchange/)
initiatives.statut_detail: Dead/Inactive (official site domain squatted by unrelated casino review as of 2026; ICO in 2018, token listed but $0 volume/price on CMC; no recent activity post-2018 ICO despite 2025-2026 listings; team moved on per LinkedIn)
initiatives.funding: $4.2M raised in ICO 2018 (target $2.8M-$20M across phases); no VC investors noted. [LiveCoinWatch](https://www.livecoinwatch.com/price/Streamity-STM), [ICO Hot List](https://www.icohotlist.com/ico/streamity/)
initiatives.traction: Circulating supply ~36M STM (out of 180M); traded on Mercatox (inactive pairs); YouTube channel 70 subs, 9 videos (old); no known users, revenue, partnerships active in 2025-2026. [CoinMarketCap](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/streamity/), [CoinLore](https://www.coinlore.com/coin/streamity/exchanges)
initiatives.raison_echec: No critical mass (near-zero trading volume/activity since ICO), bear market/crypto winter 2018, domain loss indicating abandonment, failed to deliver full roadmap (e.g., mobile app 2019, investment resource), competition from better DEXs like Uniswap.
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Secure domain/IP early; build MVP and achieve liquidity/users before heavy token launch; survive bear markets with real utility/partnerships; VRM/intention features need clear differentiation as plain DEX lacks data sovereignty hook; focus on execution over ICO hype. Team persistence (founder active in crypto till 2022) but no sustained traction.

initiatives.concept: Decentralized data economy via polyPod app: edge computing on user devices keeps personal data local and sovereign; users control data sharing (donate/rent), earn from leasing compute/data; cooperative model for citizens, foundation for regulation, enterprise for business services; GDPR-compliant, sustainable alternative to data monopolies.
initiatives.statut_detail: Likely inactive/struggling (no recent news post-2022, no official website active, low visibility despite LinkedIn presence)
initiatives.funding: No public VC funding found; cooperative shares sold to members (min €5/share)
initiatives.traction: 11-50 employees (per profiles); partnerships explored (e.g. Mercedes Benz pilots mentioned 2021); no known user/revenue metrics
initiatives.raison_echec: No critical mass achieved; too early for edge/personal data economy; adoption barriers (user onboarding, business switch from cloud giants); lack of visible product launch success post-2021 polyPod rollout plan
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Decentralized/personal data sovereignty requires massive network effects and seamless UX to compete with established cloud/data giants; cooperative funding insufficient for scaling traction; need strong pilots/partnerships early for validation

initiatives.concept: HAT (Hub-of-All-Things) Microserver: decentralized personal data server (Personal Data Account) enabling individuals to own, control, store, process, license, and share their personal data with apps/websites on their terms (what, for what purpose, how long), implementing VRM/personal data sovereignty by giving users direct control over data sharing and monetization without central silos, evolving into Dataswyft Wallet for self-sovereign data.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active
initiatives.funding: £1.2m UK government grant (HAT project, 2013); £1.8m (~$2.3m) seed round (Sep 2019, led by IQ Capital, with Pacific & Orient Properties, Alphanumeric Corp, Delin Ventures) [SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/dataswift-raises-2-million-seed-funding-revolutionize-personal-data-sharing/), [Cambridge Network](https://www.cambridgenetwork.co.uk/news/personal-data-technology-platform-dataswift-raises-ps16m-seed-funding)
initiatives.traction: No public user/revenue figures; partnerships incl. Kuza (inclusive finance), GLEIF vLEI Hackathon recognition (2025); leadership expansions (Jul 2025); active in digital trust forums; Dataswyft Network rolling out data ecosystems in multiple countries; >£33m follow-on grants from HAT research [dataswyft.com founder history](https://dataswyft.com/founderceohistory), [2025 review](https://dataswyft.com/network-news/dataswyfts-2025-year-in-review)
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (active)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Proven longevity (10+ years from 2013 research) via evolution from research (HAT microserver) to commercial infrastructure (Dataswyft Wallet), focusing on verifiable trust, policy/gov engagement, ecosystem partnerships over mass adoption; regulatory alignment (GDPR/HIPAA) and global expansion key to sustaining data sovereignty plays without critical mass dependency.

initiatives.concept: App aggregating personal data (social, photos initially; later health, finance) into user-controlled local library on device/personal cloud. Users grant explicit permissioned access to businesses via digital contracts specifying data shared, usage, retention; Digi.me acts as intermediary without seeing/holding data. Evolved to Dutch PGO health records vault with sharing controls.
initiatives.statut_detail: Pivoted: Active in 2025-2026 as Dutch MedMij-certified personal health record app by World Data Exchange B.V.; UK entity dissolved 2026
initiatives.funding: ~$10M+ pre-2017 (Series A $6.1M Swiss Re 2016, Omidyar Network $1M); merged with Personal ($16M+); acquired by World Data Exchange
initiatives.traction: 400k users in 140 countries (2016); current Dutch app active with subscriptions, integrations (Apple Health, Fitbit, MedMij), ongoing updates to 2025
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (pivoted successfully to regulated health data niche)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Pivoting to regulated verticals like national health data standards (MedMij) can sustain VRM concepts post broad-market struggles; focus on compliance (GDPR/MedMij) and partnerships (wearables, providers) for traction; acquisition enables continuation under new entity.
initiatives.sources: [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2016/06/30/digi-me-bags-6-1m-to-put-users-in-the-driving-seat-for-sharing-personal-data/), [TechCrunch Merger](https://techcrunch.com/2017/08/17/digi-me-and-personal-merge/), [Digi.me Site](https://digi.me/), [Google Play](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=me.digi.apps), [Companies House DIGI.ME LTD](https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/13632195), [Julian Ranger LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/julianranger), [Digi.me Changelog](https://digi.me/new), [Privacy Policy](https://digi.me/legal)

initiatives.concept: Zero-party personal data platform where users own and store their data on-device, control sharing for rewards (cash, discounts, donations), personal AI insights; businesses access ethical, consented real-time insights via marketplace or bespoke integrations, processing on-edge for privacy.
initiatives.statut_detail: Pivoted (app/platform shuttered ~2024; team focused on DataSapien using same tech as B2B SDK; company active as of 2025 accounts)
initiatives.funding: ~£1.4M-£1.5M seed (2021, North East Venture Fund/Mercia, 1818 Venture Capital, angels); prior/equiv ~$2M reported; earlier angel rounds mentioned
initiatives.traction: Peak: 500k+ users in 150+ countries, 4.2-4.5* app rating, 7.5M+ data exchanges, 350+ data types, blue-chip/govt clients; partnerships w/ agencies/brands for insights/prototyping
initiatives.raison_echec: Too early for mass B2C adoption (privacy/AI not mainstream); pivoted multiple times (privacy controls -> data exchange -> insights); shifted to B2B via DataSapien for scalability post-500k users
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Validate mass-market demand before heavy B2C investment; pivot to B2B licensing/SDK model for proven tech when critical mass plateaus; focus on enterprise traction in privacy-first era
initiatives.sources: [CitizenMe About](https://citizenme.com/about/), [CitizenMe Hello World](https://citizenme.com/2024/10/07/hello-world/), [Companies House CITIZENME LIMITED](https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/10971198), [DataIQ StJohn Deakins](https://www.dataiq.global/dataiq100/stjohn-deakins-founder-and-chief-executive-citizenme/), [MrWeb Evolution](https://www.mrweb.com/drno/news39593.htm), [Mercia Funding](https://www.uktechnews.info/2021/10/25/citizenme-secures-1-5-million-seed-follow-on-investment-from-mercia/), [Greenbook Interview](https://www.greenbook.org/insights/interview-with-stjohn-deakins-founder-ceo-of-citizenme), [DataSapien About](https://datasapien.com/about/)

initiatives.concept: Personal data management platform and marketplace enabling users to gain full control and transparency over their online personal data, analyze it for insights (e.g., finances, health, sustainability, carbon footprint), and selectively share or monetize it with companies (e.g., for market research, earning cashback or money). Data stored encrypted on user devices; implements VRM/intention economy via user-controlled data sharing and marketplace for data deals.
initiatives.statut_detail: Dead (ceased operations June 2024)
initiatives.funding: Raised undisclosed amounts; key investors include Occident Group AG (lead), Hemex AG, and six other private investors (likely angels via SICTIC); no total amount or detailed rounds found.
initiatives.traction: Partnerships with Consumer Panel Services GfK (Switzerland/Germany market research), Deutsche Bank (testing phase); user traction/revenue not publicly quantified; mobile app available.
initiatives.raison_echec: Lack of capital/funding in rigid VC landscape for early-stage startups; unable to secure sufficient funds despite promising idea, team, and recent partnerships.
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Even with timely mission (data sovereignty amid AI boom), user-centric data marketplaces struggle to achieve critical mass and funding without strong network effects or regulatory tailwinds; focus on B2B-first or hybrid models to build traction before consumer acquisition.

initiatives.concept: DataYogi provides personal data management tools like DataPal, a smart digital filing cabinet allowing users to store, control, and share their personal information securely on their own terms, enabling data sovereignty and selective sharing with trusted parties. Fits VRM/intention economy by empowering individuals over their data rather than organizations extracting it.
initiatives.statut_detail: Struggling/low traction (last major mentions pre-2023; possible pivot to DataPal.me active in 2026 with MyTerms launch, but unclear direct link)
initiatives.funding: No public funding found
initiatives.traction: No known users/revenue/partnerships metrics
initiatives.raison_echec: No critical mass; too early for personal data sovereignty market; lack of visible product launch or marketing post-2021 website dev
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Achieve early user traction and visible milestones; partner with established VRM ecosystems (e.g. MyData) for distribution; secure funding to scale UX and integrations before regulation mandates demand

initiatives.fondateurs: Dawn Song (Oasis Labs), Oasis Protocol Foundation team (Jernej Kos, Tadej Janež, etc.)
initiatives.concept: Privacy-first Layer-1 blockchain using TEEs for confidential smart contracts, data computation, and sovereignty. Features Sapphire confidential EVM, OPL cross-chain privacy, ROFL offchain verifiable compute. Enables users to control/monetize data in responsible data economy via encrypted processing on permissionless network.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (docs updated 2026, ROFL mainnet 2025, ongoing AI/DeFi privacy development)
initiatives.funding: $45-48M VC (a16z crypto, Polychain, Pantera, Binance Labs, Accel, etc.); $200M ecosystem fund; private sale ~$46M at $0.02/ROSE, no ICO
initiatives.traction: 7.55B ROSE circ. supply (10B max); MC ~$75-100M; TVL 12M+ ROSE liquid staking; partnerships Chainlink/Binance; hackathons 150+ submissions; Sapphire dApps (Fountain, Bit Protocol); active trading ~$0.01
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (struggling with low MC/price, KR exchanges delisting 2025 due to transparency, niche adoption)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Privacy tech viable long-term but needs strong transparency/comms to avoid regulatory/exchange issues; focus on developer adoption/utility (AI/DeFi) for critical mass beyond hype; ecosystem funds key for dApps.

initiatives.concept: Decentralized cloud computing marketplace on blockchain for on-demand access to CPU/GPU power, datasets, apps. Enables data sovereignty via confidential computing (TEE), data ownership/monetization in marketplace. Workers stake RLC; off-chain compute verified by PoCo. Evolved to privacy tools for AI/DeFi (2025+).
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (2025-2026: roadmaps, product launches like Confidential Token, Arbitrum integration, partnerships)
initiatives.funding: $12M ICO (2017, ~10k BTC, 69% of 87M RLC); self-funded since; allocations: 17.2% team/advisors, 6.9% contingency/R&D
initiatives.traction: 40k+ Privacy Pass users; partnerships (Intel Gold, NVIDIA, CCC, Arbitrum); >99% uptime; 1M RLC dev fund; hackathons; token trading $0.41 (Apr 2026), ~$30M MCAP, $15M daily vol. No public revenue/TVL.
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (ongoing project)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Sustained pivot to high-demand niches like confidential AI/DePIN privacy pays off; fixed-supply utility token with real compute demand resists inflation; self-funding post-ICO enables long-term focus; network effects from devs/partners key to traction despite low liquidity challenges.

initiatives.concept: Decentralized data brokerage platform initially on MultiversX (Elrond), enabling users to own, tokenize personal data as Data NFTs (perpetual FT or subscription LE), trade on Data DEX marketplace, with Data Coalition DAOs for governance, NFMe ID for identity, aiming for data sovereignty in Web3/Metaverse/AI. Expanded omnichain (Solana, Sui, Base), focusing on music/gaming data.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (2026): Pivoted from MultiversX V0-V2 (EOS Nov2025-Jan2026 due to low usage), focusing on Aithra infra on Solana+; recent partnerships (Walrus 2025); 100k+ community.
initiatives.funding: $7.5M total: $1.5M seed (Morningstar Ventures/Elrond Dubai Incubator), $4M private ($0.02), $2M IDO Maiar Launchpad Mar2022 ($0.04, MultiversX). Investors: Spark Digital Capital, Mechanism Capital, 10+ others.
initiatives.traction: 100,000+ contributors/community; staking 40% APR; Data DEX/NFMe on MultiversX/Solana (sunsetting UI low usage); bridge MultiversX-Solana; Sigma Music app; partnerships Walrus, Cointelegraph Accelerator, Sony x Astar; low token trading volume ~$12/day, MCAP ~$0.6M (Apr2026).
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (active, but low traction on MultiversX led to sunsetting older versions; struggling token value -99% from IDO).
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Multi-chain expansion reduces lock-in risk but fragments liquidity/user base; prioritize UX/rewards to avoid low usage sunsets; burning/tokenomics key for value accrual in niche data/AI markets; early incubator support helps bootstrap but needs sustained PMF for critical mass.

initiatives.concept: Decentralized data exchange protocol on blockchain enabling data providers to tokenize datasets via DataTokens (ERC-20 access keys) and Data NFTs (ERC-721/725y for IP), monetize on Ocean Market using AMMs for dynamic pricing, and share privately via Compute-to-Data (remote computation without data exposure), ensuring data sovereignty, privacy, and ownership while fueling AI through secure marketplaces.[Ocean Protocol site](https://oceanprotocol.com), [IQ.wiki](https://iq.wiki/wiki/bruce-pon)
initiatives.statut_detail: Active and independent in 2025-2026: Exited ASI Alliance (merger with Fetch.ai/SingularityNET) in Oct 2025 to refocus on data sovereignty/DataFi; active development (Ocean Nodes partnerships e.g. NetMind AI, product updates); official site/GitHub live; OCEAN token trading ~$0.12-0.30, buybacks/burns announced.[Ocean blog 2025](https://blog.oceanprotocol.com/ocean-protocol-product-update-2025-half-year-check-in-49adcf092d87), [Binance](https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/30786111832177), [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ocean-protocol-exits-asi-alliance-134338791.html)
initiatives.funding: $43M+ via token sales: Seed $4.2M ($0.09), Private $21.4M ($0.23), Public/IEO $8.6M ($0.12); VC ~$5M; $140M OCEAN grants via OceanDAO (2021); investors: Digital Currency Group, Fabric Ventures, Outlier Ventures, Cypher Capital.[Chainbroker](https://chainbroker.io/projects/ocean-protocol/), [CoinCarp](https://www.coincarp.com/currencies/ocean-protocol/project-info/), [Ocean press](https://oceanprotocol.com/press/2021-10-07-ocean-protocol-foundation-announces-140M-USD)
initiatives.traction: Partnerships: Johnson & Johnson, Roche, Unilever, NetMind AI (2025); active GitHub/official site; Ocean Market for data trading; Ocean Nodes GPU compute; TVL/revenue/users not prominently reported (niche DeFi/data); ongoing product updates 2025.[Ocean Foundation](https://oceanprotocol.com/about-us/foundation), [CoinCarp](https://www.coincarp.com/currencies/ocean-protocol/project-info/), [Ocean blog 2025](https://blog.oceanprotocol.com/ocean-protocol-product-update-2025-half-year-check-in-49adcf092d87)
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A - project active post-ASI exit
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Prioritize data sovereignty/privacy (Compute-to-Data) in AI ecosystems; avoid dilutive mergers without community alignment (e.g. token swaps); focus on niche (tokenized data/AI infra) over broad AI alliances; sustain via grants/partnerships but ensure token utility (staking/governance/market fees).[Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ocean-protocol-exits-asi-alliance-134338791.html), [Binance](https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/30786111832177)
initiatives.sources: [Ocean Protocol](https://oceanprotocol.com), [Foundation](https://oceanprotocol.com/about-us/foundation), [IQ.wiki Bruce Pon](https://iq.wiki/wiki/bruce-pon), [Chainbroker funding](https://chainbroker.io/projects/ocean-protocol/), [CoinCarp](https://www.coincarp.com/currencies/ocean-protocol/project-info/), [Ocean blog 2025](https://blog.oceanprotocol.com/ocean-protocol-product-update-2025-half-year-check-in-49adcf092d87), [Binance ASI exit](https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/30786111832177), [Yahoo ASI exit](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ocean-protocol-exits-asi-alliance-134338791.html), [Gate.com concept](https://www.gate.com/learn/articles/ocean-protocol-a-decentralized-data-marketplace/2747)

initiatives.fondateurs: Henri Pihkala, Nikke Nylund, Risto Karjalainen, Michael Malka
initiatives.concept: Decentralized peer-to-peer network for real-time data broadcasting and streaming, implementing data ownership through cryptographic signing and end-to-end encryption of streams; features a marketplace for buying/selling data streams (e.g., sensors, price feeds, Data Unions); uses Ethereum/Polygon for identity, permissions, and DATA token payments to incentivize node operators for secure, scalable data relay.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (Streamr 1.0 released; Streamr App for P2P video; ongoing quarterly transparency reports through Q4 2025; active development of node incentives and live video streaming)
initiatives.funding: $30.7M raised via 2017 ICO/token sale (30M CHF from 1,300+ contributors); ongoing token minting/sales for development (e.g., $457k Q1 2025, $315k Q4 2025); no traditional VC rounds noted
initiatives.traction: ~160-200 active node operators; ~93-120M DATA staked/locked in Sponsorships (~$100k-$1M TVL at $0.001 price); 6k+ streams; protocol fees 0.2-0.4M DATA/quarter; monthly node rewards ~2.5M DATA; partnerships e.g., DIMO; declining but sustained network activity into 2026
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (project active)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Sustained activity post-ICO requires continuous token incentives for operators, application-layer products (e.g., P2P video app) to drive demand, and transparent financial reporting to maintain community trust amid low token price and operator churn.

initiatives.fondateurs: Guy Zyskind (SCRT Labs), Tor Bair (Secret Foundation), from Enigma (Can Kisagun)
initiatives.concept: Secret Network is a layer-1 blockchain built on Cosmos SDK that enables privacy-preserving smart contracts (Secret Contracts). It uses Trusted Execution Environments (TEE) like Intel SGX for encrypted inputs, outputs, and state, providing programmable privacy. This allows decentralized apps to handle confidential data, enhancing data sovereignty by keeping user data private by default on-chain, unlike public blockchains. While not explicitly a data marketplace, it supports private data processing and marketplaces through confidential DeFi, NFTs, and AI applications.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active - Ongoing development with 2025/2026 roadmaps focusing on security (MRENCLAVE, Proof-of-Cloud), scalability, SecretAI/SecretVM for confidential AI, partnerships (e.g., Unbound Science, Cintara), recent upgrades (v1.15+), and ecosystem growth despite low TVL and some exchange delistings like KuCoin in 2026. Official site and docs active as of 2026.
initiatives.funding: $411.5M total raised per CryptoRank: Enigma ICO $45M (2017, settled with SEC), Founding round $11.5M (2021, Arrington Capital etc.), $400M ecosystem fund/accelerator (2022, CoinFund, Alameda Research, HashKey, Dragonfly, DeFiance Capital, Hashed, Fenbushi +18 others). Key investors: HashKey Capital, Dragonfly, Arrington Capital, CoinFund.
initiatives.traction: TVL: $2.19M (DeFiLlama 2026), Market Cap: ~$25-60M (fluctuating, ~$0.08/SCRT in early 2026), 24h volume ~$2M; Stablecoins Mcap $1.59M (SILK); Protocols: Shade ($3M TVL), SecretSwap, Sienna; Partnerships: Unbound Science (DeSci), Cintara (AI agents), Unstoppable Domains (.secret); Active devs/community (HackSecret, grants); Historical peak TVL/volume higher (>$1B trading vol past). Low current user metrics available, but ongoing AI/TEE adoption push.
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A - project active
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Prioritize robust privacy tech (TEE+encryption) for data sovereignty use cases like confidential AI/DeFi; Cosmos interoperability aids adoption but requires sustained scalability/security upgrades amid TEE vulnerabilities; Massive ecosystem funding ($400M+) fuels growth, but TVL/user traction demands killer apps; Navigate regs (post-ICO SEC) via decentralization/community governance; Token utility in staking/fees critical despite privacy coin pressures/delistings.

initiatives.concept: Brave is a privacy-focused browser that blocks third-party ads and trackers by default. BAT (Basic Attention Token), an ERC-20 token on Ethereum with a fixed supply of 1.5B, powers a new ad ecosystem: Users opt-in to privacy-preserving ads, earning ~70% of ad revenue in BAT based on anonymized attention metrics (BAM - time/pixels viewed on-device). Users own/control their data (no tracking/cookies shared), tip BAT to creators, or use in Web3/DApps. Advertisers buy BAT for campaigns; Brave takes ~30% cut; publishers get remainder based on user attention. Monetizes attention fairly without invasive data collection.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active and growing (109M+ MAU, 47M DAU in Jan 2026; Brave Search 1.6B+ queries/month; ongoing BAT developments like Rewards 3.0, .brave domains, Brave Games)
initiatives.funding: ~$252M+ total VC funding across multiple seed/series rounds (e.g., $7M seed 2016 from Founders Fund, Pantera Capital, Digital Currency Group; $50M Series A-7 2022 at $980M valuation); $35M BAT ICO 2017 (sold 1B tokens in 30s)
initiatives.traction: 109M monthly active users (Jan 2026, up from 100M Sep 2025); 47M daily active users; 100M+ Android downloads; Brave Search: 1.6B+ monthly queries; Revenue: ~$27M est. 2023, growing; 1.5B BAT supply fully circulating; partnerships with creators (Washington Post, Guardian), custodians (Uphold, Gemini), Web3 (Unstoppable Domains, Solana)
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A - thriving project
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: User-owned attention/data models can scale massively (100M+ users) with opt-in incentives and privacy-by-default; integrate utility tokens deeply into core product for retention/engagement; VC + ICO hybrid funding works; focus on on-device ML/metrics avoids regulatory pitfalls while delivering value to all parties (users earn, creators paid fairly, advertisers better ROAS).

initiatives.concept: Decentralized privacy-focused metasearch engine powered by community nodes that anonymizes searches and prevents tracking/profiling. Rewards users with PRE tokens for searches (up to ~0.01-0.1 PRE/search, stakeable for more), node operation, and referrals. Monetizes attention via keyword staking (advertisers stake PRE for ad priority) and takeover ads, giving users data ownership by not collecting/selling data and enabling token earnings from participation.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (launched Presearch 3.0 in Sep 2025 as first fully Web3 search engine; Vision 2026 roadmap; token migration planned Q2 2026; recent partnerships/revenue growth)
initiatives.funding: $20.5M-$35.5M via multi-round token sales/ICO 2017 (e.g., $1.5M+$3M+$6M+$10M+$15M early adopter sales); ~$850k prior equity; 2025 Wefunder SAFE raise $183k+ targeting $1.2M at $15M cap (acquired/restructured 2023 by Tim Enneking team)
initiatives.traction: 150k+ monthly active users; 10-13M monthly searches; 40k+ community nodes; revenue $208k annualized (up from $27k, tripling MoM); partnerships (NVIDIA Inception, Coindesk, Messiah Network, Verdicti Ventures); NFT node licenses raised 4M tokens (Dec 2025)
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (ongoing success post-restructure)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Decentralized models can sustain via token incentives for users/nodes but require iteration (e.g., 2023 acquisition/restructure, token migration) to adapt to Web3/AI trends; privacy+rewards build niche loyalty but scaling vs giants needs strong infra/partnerships; buybacks/burns tie revenue to token value effectively.

initiatives.concept: Browser extension and app for data union: users share opted-in, non-personal browsing data (history, searches) pooled and sold to businesses; earn passive rewards (points/$SWASH) redeemable for crypto/cash/gift cards while retaining privacy controls and ownership. [Swash](https://swashapp.io), [CoinGecko](https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/swash).
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (V3 launched Feb 2025 w/ mobile app; extension v3.1.2 May 2025; $16M+ earnings distributed). [Swash V3](https://swashapp.io/blog/swash-v3-everything-you-need-to-know/), [Chrome-stats](https://chrome-stats.com/d/cmndjbecilbocjfkibfbifhngkdmjgog).
initiatives.funding: $11.7M-$19M total (6 rounds incl. ~$12M IDO 2021); pre-seed €50k; investors: Outlier Ventures. Total supply 1B $SWASH. [ICODrops](https://icodrops.com/swash/), [RootData](https://www.rootdata.com/Projects/detail/Swash?k=NjYy), [Outlier](https://outlierventures.io/article/why-we-invested-in-swash/).
initiatives.traction: 220k+ users (2022); Chrome ext: 5k ratings (4.74/5); $16.8M earnings dist.; 5.8k offers; Ads: 30k users, 6.7B ads. Token MC ~$1.8M-$3M, $85k vol. Partners: Adshares, Brox.AI. [Site](https://swashapp.io), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rezanaeeni), [Blog](https://swashapp.io/blog/daos-and-data-unions-two-sides-of-the-same-coin/).
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (active, though token down 99% from ATH due to low demand/network effects).
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Proves browser-based data unions can scale users/revenue ($16M dist., 220k+ users) w/ privacy focus & diverse redemptions; succeed via PMF/product iter (V3/mobile). But tokenomics fragile sans strong utility/growth; prioritize sustainable earnings over speculation for Eigen-like infra.

initiatives.fondateurs: Daniel Bland, Michael Luckhoo, Samartha Nagabhushanam
initiatives.concept: Cirus Foundation is a blockchain-based ecosystem enabling users to own, manage, and monetize their personal data. Users install the Cirus Device (WiFi router) or Chrome extension to collect granular data (browsing, IoT, apps) with privacy controls via Data Key Wallet. Data is processed into profiles and sold on centralized/decentralized markets (Ad-Tech, Ocean Protocol), generating passive income paid in CIRUS tokens to users' wallets. Evolved to browser extension for surfing and earning crypto rewards by structuring data for AI in a decentralized network.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (micro-cap project with website, socials, low trading volume, ongoing Stage 2 milestones announced 2024, token listed, pivoted from hardware to browser/AI data)
initiatives.funding: Raised ~$2-3M total: Private rounds ~$1.89M, IDO $100K (Aug 2021 at $0.13); strategic backers incl. Jun Capital, AU21, Kosmos VC, MANTRA, Inuis Capital, Peak Capital, ExNetwork Capital, MM Ventures
initiatives.traction: Early: 4,000+ devices deployed (2021 sandbox); Soft launch: 10K downloads, 5K active users, avg revenue/user $0.55-1 (tx fees); AI app JV 225K active users (50% stake); Shift partnership (40M users/59 exchanges); low token trading vol ~$60K/24h; market cap ~$13K-$160K
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (struggling micro-cap with 99% token drop from ATH, low liquidity/market cap, community scam concerns, but website active)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Prioritize sustainable tokenomics and clear path to critical user mass/revenue beyond early traction; hardware distribution challenging—software/browser pivots easier but compete in crowded DeFi/data spaces; partner with large exchanges/ISPs for distribution; maintain community engagement to avoid rug-pull perceptions.

initiatives.concept: Web3 advertising platform that empowers users to own and monetize their personal data/attention by granting explicit permission to advertisers via "Permission Ads" and earning ASK cryptocurrency tokens as rewards for opting in and sharing data, while providing brands with compliant, high-engagement audiences.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (launched ASK on Base L2 in Jan 2025, partnerships like Impact/impact.com in 2025, website operational with evolved offerings including family AI integration)
initiatives.funding: Token sale/public distribution (ASK token with 100B total supply; allocations: 45% ecosystem growth, 30% purchasers/supporters, 10% developers/advisors, 15% team). No traditional VC funding details found.
initiatives.traction: Over 700,000 unique wallets holding ASK (as of platform launch era, ongoing trading); 350,000+ users and $45M in rewards distributed (per 2025 partnership announcement); ASK trading on Gate.io, Uniswap (Polygon), Aerodrome (Base) with ~$9K daily volume; partnerships with impact.com (2025), Impact Partner Network.
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (project active and developing)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Demonstrates viability of user-owned data monetization through token incentives and permission-based ads; success factors include blockchain integration (Polygon/Base/Eth), regulatory compliance (GDPR/CCPA), strategic partnerships for distribution, and evolution (e.g., AI/family apps) while maintaining core token utility; low token price post-ATH highlights need for sustained ecosystem growth and mass adoption to achieve critical mass.

initiatives.fondateurs: Michael Dillhyon and colleagues from Swiss Federal Institute of Technology
initiatives.concept: Swiss cooperative (emulating Visa's model) where members own and govern the platform. Users store health/genomic data securely, retain full control over access/sharing (with providers, researchers, family), get rewarded for research contributions. Co-op owns data collectively; one-member-one-vote governance; leverages Swiss privacy laws for neutrality/trust. Operating subsidiary handles services like app data storage.
initiatives.statut_detail: Likely inactive (website down since ~2020; no recent news/mentions post-2019; founder moved to US, no current Healthbank role listed)
initiatives.funding: Venture funding incl. Series A ~$2.8M (Palmarium); STO for HBE token (target CHF 27.6M at CHF 0.0143/token, 55% supply; 2019); other angels/VCs (e.g. Angel Investment Network)
initiatives.traction: 270k members (2019); partnerships (UKBB Basel Univ Children's Hospital 2014 for research); early revenue from app data storage (not profitable 2019)
initiatives.raison_echec: No critical mass achieved post-270k (2019); too early for market (regulatory hurdles for health data sharing, privacy laws); crypto winter hit STO; website offline; lack of sustained updates/traction
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Build critical mass early via incentives/partnerships; navigate regs (GDPR-like) before scaling; avoid over-reliance on tokens in volatile markets; ensure UX/cultural trust for data sharing; have sustainable non-token revenue (storage/services)

initiatives.fondateurs: Sam De Brouwer, Walter De Brouwer
initiatives.concept: Enterprise AI platform using edge computing, zero-trust architecture, and privacy-preserving infrastructure to process personal health/genomic data (lab results, biomarkers) on-device, enabling conversational AI for personalized insights without centralizing sensitive data. Early blockchain integration (Neurons token) for decentralized data timestamping and rewarding participation in digital health trials/research, giving individuals ownership/control over their data while unlocking value for research.
initiatives.statut_detail: Acquired by Sharecare (Feb 2021); technology integrated into Sharecare platform; active as part of Sharecare (NASDAQ: SHCR) in 2026
initiatives.funding: $41M total (~$2.3M crypto presale/ICO for Neurons token 2017 led by Pantera Capital; seed from Comet Labs, S2 Capital, F50, Legend Star; $100M/5yr Anthem deal); terms of Sharecare acq undisclosed
initiatives.traction: 80+ clients (payers/pharma/providers); key partnerships: Anthem ($100M deal, Passport app), UCB Biopharma, Deloitte; 70+ engineers; pre-acq revenue ~$1.9M; products: robo-genomics NLP modules, consumer app for personal-omics trials
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (successful acquisition exit)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Privacy-first edge AI + early blockchain/token incentives can attract major payers (Anthem) and lead to strategic exits (Sharecare), but token utility must pivot to enterprise licensing for scale; demonstrates viability of data sovereignty via federated/zero-trust models without full decentralization.

initiatives.fondateurs: George Church, Dennis Grishin, Kamal Obbad
initiatives.concept: Nebula Genomics empowers individuals with ownership of their genomic data through whole-genome sequencing and blockchain technology. Users sequence their full genome (30x coverage for ~$299), store data privately, control access via smart contracts and encryption (e.g., Secure Enclaves, homomorphic encryption), monetize by selling/renting anonymized access to researchers/pharma for Nebula tokens or subsidies, unlike competitors owning data.
initiatives.statut_detail: Acquired by ProPhase Labs in 2021 for $14.6M; restructured under ProPhase (now profitable, subscription model, 16PB dataset ~150M equiv. tests from 130 countries); genomics lab shut Feb 2025; site active but up for sale (May 2025), rebranded partly to DNA Complete; operational in 2026 per reviews.
initiatives.funding: $4.3M seed/Series A (2018, led by Khosla Ventures; investors: Arch Venture, F-Prime, Mayfield, Fenbushi Capital, etc.); ~$5M total pre-acq; acquired 2021 for $14.6M (cash/stock); planned Nebula token (no ICO executed).
initiatives.traction: 16-petabyte dataset (equiv. 150M ancestry tests, 130 countries); >350 health/ancestry reports; subscription revenue (strong renewal margins); partnerships: Oasis Labs (privacy), BGI (sequencing), Veritas Genetics (platform); reviews indicate users but delays; one of largest DTC WGS providers pre-acq.
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A - operational under ProPhase, shifted from blockchain token focus to subscription WGS DTC; challenges: processing delays, privacy lawsuit (2024 GIPA violation), lab shutdown, sale process.
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Prioritize robust privacy tech (blockchain/enclaves) from day one to build trust; enable user monetization to bootstrap data scale; partner for low-cost sequencing; subscription > one-off/token volatility; large diverse datasets highly valuable (16PB attractive despite pivots); avoid over-reliance on crypto amid regulatory/UX hurdles.

initiatives.fondateurs: Dawn Barry (President), Bob Kain (CEO), David Lewis (CFO)
initiatives.concept: LunaDNA is a community-owned platform where individuals upload their genomic, health, and real-world data (e.g., from 23andMe, EHRs, wearables) to earn ownership shares in the company. Data is de-identified and sold/queried by researchers and pharma for medical research; users share in profits via dividends, with full control, privacy, transparency, and ability to withdraw consent/data.
initiatives.statut_detail: Dead (ceased operations end of January 2024; no activity in 2025-2026)
initiatives.funding: $12.4M total raised from VCs including ARCH Venture Partners, Illumina Ventures, Bain Capital Ventures, F-Prime Capital, Osage Venture Partners; rounds include seed (~$2-3M in 2017-18), $4.6M in 2019 (bringing total to ~$7.6M); no ICO/token (equity shares, SEC-qualified; not crypto despite early mentions)
initiatives.traction: Partnerships with Biogen, Genetic Alliance (PEER platform, 50k+ participants), National Society of Genetic Counsellors, PXE International, AliveAndKickn, Medfusion (EHR), Genomelink; ~35 patient orgs; 10 patents filed; revenue growing pre-shutdown but expenses exceeded revenues (no cash June 2022); user # unknown
initiatives.raison_echec: Unable to secure next funding round amid biotech downturn despite growing revenue and nearing profitability; expenses chronically exceeded revenues, ran out of cash (per 2022 SEC filing)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Proves demand for individual data ownership/profit-sharing models (pioneered patient-centric research data platforms, partnerships with pharma/patient groups) but highlights funding vulnerability in biotech VC winters; need sustainable path to critical mass/revenue before capital dries up; equity-share model innovative but complex regulatory/liquidity hurdles vs simpler token models

initiatives.fondateurs: Gregory Rockson, Daniel Shoukimas, James Finucane
initiatives.concept: mPharma is a technology-driven healthcare company providing vendor-managed inventory (VMI) for pharmacies, retail pharmacy operations via mutti brand, telehealth (mutti Doctor), and data insights through Bloom platform. In health data context, it collects personal and medical data (incl. biometrics) with user consent for operations, research, marketing; offers withdrawal/access rights and security measures (encryption, pseudonymization). Aligns loosely with data sovereignty via purpose-limited processing and user controls, but company owns/uses aggregated anonymized data for supply chain optimization/market intelligence, not true personal data ownership or user-controlled VRM/intention economy (no user-directed vendor selection or intent-based data sharing like Apple Privacy). Not Salesforce Customer 360-related; standard centralized data model where mPharma manages relationships/supply, not empowering individual VRM.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (recent 2025 investments from GIP, partnerships, leadership transition, operations in multiple African countries incl. Ghana)
initiatives.funding: $88.53M+ raised across 12 rounds; investors incl. CDC Group, Breyer Capital, JAM Fund, Growth Investment Partners (GIP) (2025), Citibank debt ([CBInsights](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/mpharma/financials), [TechCrunch 2021](https://techcrunch.com/2021/10/11/mpharma-a-telehealth-pioneer-out-of-ghana-gets-physical-with-100-virtual-centers-across-africa/))
initiatives.traction: Network of 300+ pharmacies across 9 African countries (Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, etc.); serves 100,000+ patients/month; 150,000+ mutti members; pharmacies report 100-120% revenue growth; recent GIP investment for West Africa expansion incl. Ghana mutti network ([mPharma site](https://mpharma.com/), [60dB survey via report](https://mpharma.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/Impact-Report-_mPharma-2021.pdf), [WEF](https://www.weforum.org/organizations/mpharma/))
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A - Active and growing
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: mPharma shows successful health data use for supply chain efficiency/personalized care in emerging markets, but highlights limits of centralized models for true intention economy: users consent but lack granular control over data flows/usage (e.g., marketing/research). For MYNε/Eigen, prioritize user-owned data vaults with verifiable intent signals for VRM, avoiding platform aggregation to enable sovereignty; integrate privacy-by-design like Apple while scaling pharmacy networks.

initiatives.fondateurs: Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, Ronald Wayne
initiatives.concept: Apple's privacy initiatives (e.g., App Tracking Transparency (ATT, 2021: opt-in for cross-app tracking), Intelligent Tracking Prevention (ITP, 2017+), Private Cloud Compute (PCC, 2024+: stateless AI processing inaccessible to Apple), on-device AI, end-to-end encryption) enhance data sovereignty via user consents, anti-tracking, no third-party marketing sharing. Relates to intention economy by enabling user control over data flows but not truly it: no user sovereign VRM tools for direct vendor intention signaling; Apple remains gatekeeper/controller per policy. Vs. Salesforce Customer 360 (vendor CRM, not customer-VRM).
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (expanding 2025-2026 with PCC across platforms, privacy dashboard; UK ADP disputes ongoing)
initiatives.funding: Public (AAPL); $500B+ US investments 2025-2029 (manufacturing, AI R&D) [Apple Newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2025/02/apple-will-spend-more-than-500-billion-usd-in-the-us-over-the-next-four-years/)
initiatives.traction: 2B+ active devices; 95%+ ATT opt-out rates reshaped ad industry; dominant premium mobile market; developer mandates (Privacy Manifests)
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (active); Not true intention economy: lacks decentralized user data ownership/VRM (personal agents for vendor relations); ecosystem lock-in, Apple as data controller [Apple Policy](https://www.apple.com/legal/privacy/en-ww/), [Searls critique](https://pxlnv.com/linklog/searls-apple-privacy/)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Prioritize true user sovereignty beyond consents: build portable data vaults/personal AI agents enabling direct P2P intention expression to vendors, avoiding platform centrism; combine privacy tech with open VRM tools for genuine intention economy.

initiatives.fondateurs: Marc Benioff, Parker Harris, Dave Moellenhoff, Frank Dominguez
initiatives.concept: Salesforce Customer 360 is a suite of CRM products providing companies with a unified 360-degree view of customer data from multiple sources, enabling sales, service, marketing teams to deliver personalized experiences. Includes Data 360 for governance, Einstein Trust Layer for privacy/security, Zero Copy for data connections without movement. Relates to data sovereignty via features supporting residency/compliance (e.g., Hyperforce zones), but is company-centric: aggregates customer data under vendor control for CRM/upsell. NOT true VRM (customer-side independence tools per Doc Searls/ProjectVRM) or intention economy (customer expresses intents to market). Mimics "360" but flips VRM to vendor advantage. Apple Privacy (ATT, App Tracking Transparency) blocks cross-app tracking for user sovereignty, closer to intention economy by preventing unwanted surveillance but lacks VRM's active engagement tools.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active
initiatives.funding: Public (NYSE: CRM); FY2025 revenue ~$38B (projected from trends)
initiatives.traction: 150K+ customers, 12% global market share CRM leader; powers 1T+ AI predictions/week; 97% ROI per surveys
initiatives.raison_echec: Not applicable; however not truly VRM/intention economy as centralizes data with vendor (Salesforce), not user. CRM extracts value from customer data without granting ownership/control/independence.
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Build true customer-side VRM: personal data pods (e.g., Solid/Inrupt) where users own/control data, express intents, negotiate terms (e.g., IEEE P7012 MyTerms). Avoid CRM traps; enable intention economy by empowering users over vendors. Data sovereignty requires user-held keys/control, not vendor promises.

initiatives.concept: Africa's leading decision intelligence company providing real-time, aggregated consumer insights from high-frequency surveys (120k interviews/year, 80M data points across 21 markets). Collects survey data with consent but aggregates/anonymizes for sale to businesses (FMCG, banks); no relation to data sovereignty/VRM/intention economy (standard market research, not user-controlled data or vendor management). Not VRM like Salesforce (business-vendor tool) or intention economy like Apple (user-expressed intents). Complies with basic privacy (Canadian focus), operates under POPIA in SA without special sovereignty features.
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (2026 podcast, website ©2025, Kasi AI launch)
initiatives.funding: ~$2.24M (2021 unattributed, undisclosed investors); revenue ~$6M (2025 est.)
initiatives.traction: 120k+ annual interviews, 80M data points, 21 African markets incl. South Africa; clients incl. Bloomberg, Refinitiv, banks/FMCGs; 2-10 employees; partnerships e.g. Creative VMLY&R
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A (active, thriving)
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Market research platforms like Kasi succeed by aggregating anonymized consumer data for B2B insights but do not empower individual data sovereignty or intention economy principles. True VRM/intention requires user-owned data pools/control (e.g. data wallets, user-negotiated terms), not company-controlled surveys. For MYNε/Eigen, prioritize P2P/user-centric models over B2B aggregation to align with sovereignty; ensure African ops comply with POPIA while building local data control to avoid extractive critiques.

initiatives.fondateurs: Elo Umeh (CEO), Ayodeji Balogun (CTO), Oduntan Odunbajo
initiatives.concept: Data and marketing tech firm using telco/mobile data, AI/ML via Terragon Marketing Cloud (CDP) for personalized B2C campaigns, insights in Africa. Claims NDPR/GDPR compliance, data anonymization/encryption. Business/telco-centric data aggregation/monetization, not user-controlled; no VRM (user-vendor mgmt) or intention economy (user signals). Unlike Salesforce C360 (CRM unification, not VRM) or Apple Privacy (user opt-outs, not intention-based).
initiatives.statut_detail: Active (live site, 2025 profiles)
initiatives.funding: $19M total: $5M Series A (2018 TLcom), $9M Series B (2023 Orange Ventures, TLcom et al.); investors incl. TLcom, Orange Ventures, LoftyInc, VestedWorld
initiatives.traction: $9.8M revenue est., 52 employees, 150M+ profiles, 7k+ businesses, partners MTN/Microsoft/Unilever/FCMB; high ROI cases (200% acquisition, 20% CTR)
initiatives.raison_echec: N/A - successful/active
initiatives.lecon_pour_myne: Differentiate via true user empowerment (ownership, granular consent, intention portability) beyond B2B martech compliance; user-first sovereignty avoids commoditization as data broker.
